__Weld Frontend Design Master System__

__Dynamic\-first Roblox Studio\-native landing page doctrine__

Version 1\.0 | April 25, 2026 | Source pack: Stage 1\-5 Overhaul Series

__Core oath  
__Weld is not a hybrid between a landing page and a web app\. It is a dynamic, Studio\-native product surface where the page itself demonstrates the product promise: noisy creator identity becomes structured, verified, scoutable proof\.

# Document Navigation Map

This master document is built to serve three audiences at once: product/design leadership, frontend engineers, and coding agents such as Codex and Claude Code\. The first third defines doctrine and taste, the middle third specifies the dynamic UI system, and the final third provides implementation snippets, QA rubrics, spreadsheet schemas, and agent prompts\.

__Purpose__

# 00\. Executive Doctrine

The Weld landing page must be designed, built, and reviewed as a dynamic product surface\. It is allowed to explain the product, but it must never behave like a generic static marketing page with decorative animation sprinkled on top\.

__Prime directive  
__Every major section must read state, emit events, respond to scroll or input, and visibly affect at least one other part of the page\. If a section only fades in with static text and cards, it fails the system\.

## What Weld is

- Roblox Studio\-native: panels, inspectors, Explorer trees, Output logs, script tabs, compact rows, and stateful tool behavior\.
- Proof\-first: shipped games, visits, role, rate, availability, verification, and response data should appear before generic persuasion\.
- Anti\-Discord chaos: not a Discord clone, but a structured alternative to cold DMs, noisy hiring channels, scammy server posts, and rate haggling\.
- Dynamic\-first: every important interaction should feel like inspecting, compiling, parsing, verifying, or claiming\.
- Agent\-safe: Codex and Claude Code should be able to implement sections without inventing style, copy, motion, or state behavior\.

## What Weld is not

- Not a dark fintech dashboard\.
- Not a creator\-economy landing page with pastel gradients and giant feature cards\.
- Not a generic terminal gimmick with green hacker text\.
- Not a Discord replacement that looks like Discord everywhere\.
- Not a Webflow template with continuous shimmer buttons\.
- Not a hybrid brochure where only the hero is interactive and the rest is static content\.

## The one\-sentence product surface

__Use this sentence in every design and engineering review  
__Weld is a dynamic, Studio\-native interface where a Roblox creator profile recompiles itself from noisy identity into verified, scoutable proof\.

## Master acceptance test

A Roblox developer should understand within the first screen that the interface was made by someone who has opened Roblox Studio, watched the Output window, navigated Explorer, written LuaU, and seen how chaotic Discord hiring can be\. That recognition matters more than abstract beauty\.

__Design pressure__

__Preferred outcome__

__Reject outcome__

First impression

Feels like Studio booting and compiling proof\.

Feels like a startup landing page with dark colors\.

Hero behavior

Boot logs, profile inspector, CTA command state, dynamic data\.

Static title, subtitle, card, email input\.

Role selection

Recompiles profile, code tab, stats, logs, and CTA\.

Highlights a pill and changes a label only\.

Discord comparison

Shows chaos being parsed into proof signals\.

Static before/after cards\.

Motion

Communicates status, verification, selection, unlocks, errors\.

Decorative parallax and looped shimmer\.

Mobile

Compact Studio\-like UI that preserves state\.

Desktop sections stacked vertically\.

# 01\. Source Packet Synthesis

The five supplied stage files are treated as the tactical source packet for the master system\. This chapter consolidates them into one unified design operating model\. Stages 1 and 2 establish the base language; Stages 3 and 4 establish the experience; Stage 5 adds memorability, native motifs, and anti\-SaaS polish\.

__Stage__

__Original focus__

__Master\-system role__

__Dynamic\-first interpretation__

Stage 1

Color System and Token Overhaul

Foundation for all surfaces, states, syntax, badges, and actions\.

Tokens are not decorative; they communicate state: selected, verified, available, warning, ready, and claimed\.

Stage 2

LuaU Code Aesthetic and Typography

Transforms terminal styling into Studio Output, LuaU editor, and Explorer density\.

Text surfaces must be active interfaces: code tabs type, logs append, rows select, and values recompile\.

Stage 3

Animation System Rebuild

Defines Boot Feel, Breathing, and Compile Snap\.

Motion is state feedback\. Booting, hovering, selecting, verifying, and submitting must all change system state\.

Stage 4

Section\-by\-Section Component Overhaul

Maps every section to a Roblox\-native interface metaphor\.

Each section must become a stateful component, not a themed static block\.

Stage 5

Native Roblox Visual Motifs and Polish

Adds screenshot\-worthy Studio motifs, code watermarks, badges, stats, mobile mode, and copy audit\.

Polish must be reactive: counters animate, badges reveal, code backgrounds respond, and mobile preserves interactivity\.

## Dependency logic

1. Stage 1 must happen before any component polish because color tokens encode both brand and state\.
2. Stage 2 must happen before deep component work because typography, code blocks, Output logs, and Explorer rows establish recognition\.
3. Stage 3 must be implemented as a page\-wide motion system, not isolated one\-off animations\.
4. Stage 4 must convert every section into a metaphor\-led dynamic component\.
5. Stage 5 must be implemented after the core system so motifs enhance the experience instead of masking weak structure\.

## Primary source decisions absorbed

__Decision__

__Origin stage__

__Final master ruling__

Studio Dark palette replaces generic navy\-black\.

Stage 1

Adopted as root token system\.

Studio Blue becomes primary action/selection color\.

Stage 1

Adopted as CTA, focus, tab, Explorer selected\-row, and log\-ready color\.

LuaU syntax colors drive labels, strings, functions, types, variables, and comments\.

Stage 1 and Stage 2

Adopted as semantic and code tokens\.

Terminal output becomes Studio Output panel\.

Stage 2 and Stage 4

Adopted as hero boot and global event log\.

How It Works becomes LuaU editor tabs\.

Stage 2 and Stage 4

Adopted as dynamic script editor that updates by role/mode\.

Role chips become Studio Explorer tree\.

Stage 2 and Stage 4

Adopted as role state controller\.

Continuous shimmer is removed\.

Stage 2

Adopted\. Only use targeted glow and one\-time charge effects\.

Boot Feel, Breathing, Compile Snap define motion\.

Stage 3

Adopted as required motion taxonomy\.

Every landing section maps to a Roblox\-native UI metaphor\.

Stage 4

Adopted as anti\-generic rule\.

Luau watermarks, Studio window, visit counters, compact mobile, copy audit\.

Stage 5

Adopted with brand\-safety refinements\.

__Stage 5 brand\-safety refinement  
__The source packet suggests a native Roblox verified badge\. In the master system, use Roblox\-inspired proof language carefully\. Prefer "Roblox proof verified" or "Proof verified via Roblox OAuth" over anything that implies official Roblox endorsement\.

# 02\. Audience and Anti\-Discord Positioning

The visual direction only works if the page is emotionally precise\. Weld is for people who already ship and are tired of being forced to prove legitimacy inside noisy channels\. The design should respect that they are builders, not passive leads\.

## Primary audience

__Audience__

__What they ship__

__Pain__

__What the page must make them feel__

LuaU scripters

Systems, tools, OOP modules, DataStoreService work, economy logic, liveops systems\.

They are judged by DMs, vague screenshots, and "can you script?" messages\.

My proof is visible before I have to explain myself\.

Builders / environment artists

Maps, obbies, simulators, hangouts, world polish, lighting, optimization\.

Work is hard to summarize in a text channel\.

The page knows shipped Roblox worlds are proof\.

UI/UX designers

Roblox\-native menus, shops, flows, HUDs, icons, monetization interfaces\.

Portfolios get flattened into random image dumps\.

Weld treats interface work like structured product evidence\.

VFX / technical artists

Particles, abilities, combat effects, timing, visual feedback\.

Discord previews do not show reliability or game context\.

Effects are connected to shipped games and proof rows\.

Animators

Character motion, emotes, combat, cutscenes, interaction polish\.

The craft is reduced to "send reel"\.

The profile helps studios inspect skill fit quickly\.

Studios / scouts

Hiring pipelines and contractor searches\.

Discord hiring is noisy, slow, and trust\-poor\.

Weld gives them scoutable proof without cold\-DM chaos\.

## Anti\-Discord stance

The design should not become a rant against Discord\. Discord is a familiar source of noise, not the villain\. Weld should dramatize the difference between chaotic posts and structured proof: message noise enters, proof signals compile out\.

__Discord chaos__

__Weld response__

Cold DMs, rate haggling, vague portfolios, deleted messages, duplicate posts\.

Structured role, rate, availability, games, visits, response time, and verification\.

Trust is negotiated manually in private messages\.

Proof is inspected in the interface before contact\.

Channels reward whoever shouts most recently\.

Weld rewards shipped work and verified data\.

Studios filter by scrolling, asking, and guessing\.

Studios filter by role, rate, proof, availability, and live status\.

__Positioning line  
__Discord is where the noise happens\. Weld is where proof gets found\.

## Emotional requirements

- A Roblox developer should feel recognized, not marketed to\.
- A studio scout should feel speed and trust, not novelty for novelty's sake\.
- The anti\-Discord contrast should be sharp but not childish\.
- The page should carry confidence without venture\-backed buzzwords\.
- The copy should assume the user has already shipped something worth inspecting\.

# 03\. Dynamic\-First Product Surface

Dynamic\-first means the page behaves like a product demonstration instead of a pitch deck\. The user should not merely read claims about Weld\. The interface should perform the transformation Weld promises\.

## Definitions

__Type__

__Behavior__

__Weld ruling__

Static

The page displays content\. Animation, if present, is decorative\.

Rejected for all major sections\.

Hybrid

The hero or CTA has interaction, but most sections remain static cards/text\.

Rejected\. This is the primary failure mode to avoid\.

Dynamic\-first

The page has connected state, cross\-section effects, live logs, role changes, animated proof, and stateful CTA\.

Required\. This is the target\.

## Dynamic maturity scale

__Level__

__Description__

__Example__

__Allowed?__

0

Static content\.

A feature card fades in\.

No\.

1

Local interaction only\.

A role chip highlights itself\.

Only for minor UI\.

2

Local state affects visible content\.

Role changes the profile card\.

Minimum for small components\.

3

Cross\-section reactivity\.

Role changes profile, code, stats, logs, and CTA\.

Required for major sections\.

4

Narrative system state\.

Boot, inspect, recompile, parse, verify, claim flow is visible across the page\.

Target for final experience\.

## The dynamic narrative

__Phase__

__User experience__

__Required surfaces__

Boot

The page comes online like Studio launching\.

Output logs, flash, header materialization, profile enter, CTA ready pulse\.

Inspect

The user examines a developer profile like an object in Properties\.

Properties rows, verified values, role data, visit counters\.

Recompile

The user selects a role or mode; values scramble and resolve\.

Explorer role tree, code tabs, logs, stats, CTA copy\.

Parse

Discord noise is processed into structured proof\.

Discord chaos panel, Weld compile panel, dividing flash, output rows\.

Verify

Proof becomes visible and trusted\.

Counters, badges, OAuth proof text, proof rows, status changes\.

Claim

The final CTA behaves like a command being executed\.

Command input, validation logs, submit state, success/error badge\.

## Dynamic section requirements

- Every major section must read from shared state or emit events into shared state\.
- Every major section must have a scroll, hover, focus, click, input, or intersection behavior that changes visible state\.
- At least four sections must be cross\-linked through selectedRole and audienceMode\.
- Every interaction must append an Output log line or equivalent visible status response unless the interaction is purely cosmetic\.
- Reduced\-motion mode must preserve meaning with instant state changes and fades, not remove the interaction entirely\.

__Static section ban  
__Static feature cards, static testimonial cards, static KPI grids, static Discord screenshots, generic fade\-up reveals, and static email waitlist forms are banned unless they are rewritten as dynamic components\.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

# 04\. Global State and Interaction Architecture

The frontend should be structured around a shared state model\. The page is not a set of isolated sections\. It is one interactive interface where role, audience, proof metric, boot status, code tab, and CTA state affect multiple components\.

## Core state model

__  Core shared type model__  \[TypeScript\]

  type WeldRole = "scripter" | "builder" | "uiux" | "vfx" | "animator";

  type AudienceMode = "developer" | "studio";

  type BootStatus = "booting" | "scanning" | "ready";

  type ScriptTab = "auth" | "profile" | "discover" | "verify";

  type ProofMetric = "visits" | "games" | "badges" | "response" | "availability";

  type ClaimState = "idle" | "focused" | "typing" | "ready" | "submitting" | "success" | "error";

  

  type WeldOutputLog = \{

    id: string;

    timestamp: string;

    severity: "info" | "success" | "warn" | "error" | "ready";

    message: string;

  \};

  

  type WeldPageState = \{

    selectedRole: WeldRole;

    audienceMode: AudienceMode;

    bootStatus: BootStatus;

    activeScriptTab: ScriptTab;

    selectedProofMetric: ProofMetric;

    claimState: ClaimState;

    email: string;

    compactMode: boolean;

    reducedMotion: boolean;

    logs: WeldOutputLog\[\];

  \};

## Role data configuration

__  Config\-first data pattern__  \[TypeScript\]

  export const weldRoles = \{

    scripter: \{

      label: "Scripter",

      fileIcon: "ModuleScript",

      rate: "45 R$/hr",

      availability: "Open now",

      shippedGames: 8,

      visits: "17\.3M",

      responseTime: "2h avg",

      skills: \["LuaU", "OOP", "DataStoreService", "ProfileService"\],

      proofRows: \["Economy system shipped", "17\.3M visits verified", "3 liveops patches"\],

      ctaDeveloper: "Claim your Scripter slot",

      ctaStudio: "Scout Scripters with proof"

    \},

    builder: \{

      label: "Builder",

      fileIcon: "Folder",

      rate: "25 R$/hr",

      availability: "Sprint lane",

      shippedGames: 12,

      visits: "9\.8M",

      responseTime: "4h avg",

      skills: \["Worlds", "Lighting", "Optimization", "Map Flow"\],

      proofRows: \["12 maps shipped", "Streaming safe", "Lighting pass verified"\],

      ctaDeveloper: "Claim your Builder slot",

      ctaStudio: "Scout Builders with proof"

    \}

  \} as const;

## Event architecture

__Event__

__Origin__

__State changes__

__Visual feedback__

BOOT\_STARTED

Page mount

bootStatus = booting; logs append\.

Near\-black start, logo mark, Output panel begins\.

BOOT\_READY

Boot timeline complete

bootStatus = ready\.

CTA ready pulse, ready log line, header full opacity\.

ROLE\_SELECTED

ExplorerRoleTree click/tap

selectedRole updates; activeScriptTab may change\.

Compile flash, value scramble, code tab update, logs append\.

AUDIENCE\_SWITCHED

Header mode toggle

audienceMode updates\.

Copy perspective switches between developer and studio\.

PROOF\_METRIC\_FOCUSED

Stats hover/focus

selectedProofMetric updates\.

Related property row and code comment highlight\.

DISCORD\_PARSE\_STARTED

Discord section enters viewport

Parse progress begins\.

Discord left panel slides in, Weld right panel compiles signals\.

CLAIM\_INPUT\_CHANGED

Email input

email and claimState update\.

Command prompt brightens, validation logs update\.

CLAIM\_SUBMITTED

CTA submit

claimState = submitting/success/error\.

Button compresses, Output logs success/error, badge unlock appears\.

## Component wiring example

__  Connected component tree__  \[TSX\]

  <WeldPageProvider initialRole="scripter">

    <StudioToolbarHeader />

    <HeroSurface>

      <StudioOutputPanel />

      <DeveloperPropertiesPanel />

      <FloatingStudioWindow />

    </HeroSurface>

    <ExplorerRoleTree />

    <LuaUSyntaxEditor />

    <DiscordChaosParser />

    <ProofStatsGrid />

    <ReferralBadgeUnlocks />

    <StudioWelcomeCTA />

  </WeldPageProvider>

## State ownership rules

- Top\-level page state owns selectedRole, audienceMode, logs, claimState, and reducedMotion\.
- Component\-local state may own hover, focus, expanded row, and animation tick values only\.
- No component may hard\-code role\-specific copy that already exists in weldRoles config\.
- Output logs should be append\-only during user interactions, capped to a reasonable maximum such as 12 visible rows\.
- Reduced motion should be computed once and passed through context or a hook\.

# 05\. Cross\-Section Interaction Map

The map below is the operational heart of the dynamic\-first system\. It prevents isolated micro\-interactions by requiring each meaningful user action to create visible system\-wide consequences\.

__User action__

__Immediate effect__

__Cross\-page effect__

__Output panel response__

Select role in Explorer

Selected row highlights and expands\.

Properties, LuaU code, stats, badges, CTA, and proof rows update\.

\[12:04:02\.118\] Role filter changed: Scripter

Hover or focus proof metric

Metric glows and sparkline brightens\.

Matching Properties row and code comment highlight\.

\[12:04:02\.249\] Inspecting proof metric: visits

Switch script tab

Tab border moves to active file\.

How It Works explanation and code block content update\.

\[12:04:02\.331\] Opened weld\_profile\.luau

Toggle developer/studio mode

Toolbar mode changes\.

Copy and CTA perspective shift across page\.

\[12:04:02\.447\] Audience context switched: studio

Enter email

Input becomes command buffer\.

CTA state changes from idle to ready when valid\.

\[12:04:02\.601\] claim\_slot command ready

Submit email

Button compresses and compiles\.

Success badge unlocks; error state if invalid\.

\[12:04:02\.950\] CLAIM\_SLOT\_SUCCESS

Scroll into Discord section

Discord chaos activates\.

Weld compile panel parses messages into proof signals\.

\[12:04:03\.104\] Noise reduced to proof signals

Scroll into stats

Counters start counting\.

Related badges and proof rows become active\.

\[12:04:03\.218\] Proof metrics loaded

Open FAQ property group

Accordion row expands\.

Output logs context help if relevant\.

\[12:04:03\.402\] Inspecting FAQ group: verification

## Role selection: required recompile sequence

1. ExplorerRoleTree emits ROLE\_SELECTED with selected role key\.
2. Active row enters selected state in Studio blue at 20 percent opacity\.
3. Output panel appends role change, recompiling, and ready lines\.
4. DeveloperPropertiesPanel labels stay stable while values scramble and resolve\.
5. LuaUSyntaxEditor switches to role\-relevant script and types or snaps content depending on motion setting\.
6. ProofStatsGrid counts or snaps to the new role values\.
7. CTA copy changes to role\-specific claim/scout language\.
8. Reduced\-motion mode performs the same state updates with no scramble, no parallax, and no large transforms\.

## CTA command state machine

__  CTA state machine copy__  \[TypeScript\]

  const claimCopy = \{

    idle: "claim\_slot \-\-email=",

    focused: "command buffer active",

    typing: "validating email input",

    ready: "press Enter to claim",

    submitting: "compiling invite request",

    success: "SLOT\_CLAIMED",

    error: "INVALID\_EMAIL\_FORMAT"

  \};

__Dynamic QA question  
__After every interaction, ask: what changed elsewhere on the page? If the answer is "nothing," the interaction is too local for a dynamic\-first interface\.

# 06\. Brand Voice and Copy Rules

Weld should sound like it was written by a builder who ships, not by a SaaS marketer\. The copy should be short, technical, proof\-led, slightly rebellious, and comfortable with Roblox\-native language\. It should not over\-explain itself\.

## Voice profile

__Quality__

__Do__

__Avoid__

Technical confidence

Use concrete proof words: shipped, verified, visits, role, rate, availability\.

Use vague words: empower, unlock, seamless, revolutionary\.

Rebellious clarity

Name the problem: cold DMs, Discord noise, proof buried in channels\.

Attack users or sound childish\.

Developer\-native

Use Studio, LuaU, Output, Explorer, proof rows, compile, inspect when earned\.

Force jargon into every sentence\.

Compact

Short lines and punchy verbs\.

Long paragraph marketing claims\.

Respectful

Assume the creator has shipped something worth seeing\.

Beg the user to join a waitlist\.

## Approved verbs and nouns

__Approved verbs__

__Approved nouns__

__Power phrases__

ship, scout, verify, compile, inspect, claim, parse, filter, pull, prove

proof, roster, slot, lane, role, rate, visits, games, signal, output, profile

Your shipped games do the talking\.

unlock only for badges, not opportunities

slot instead of waitlist

Studios pull you in \- no cold DMs\.

scan, load, match, resolve, claim

developer lane, proof row, scout view, command buffer

One profile\. All the proof studios need\.

## Banned phrases

- Get discovered
- Join the waitlist
- Unlock opportunities
- Creator\-first platform
- Seamless workflow
- Revolutionary
- All\-in\-one platform
- Empower creators
- Grow your personal brand
- The future of hiring

## Copy audit table

__Generic / current line__

__Replace with__

__Reason__

Get discovered by Roblox studios\.

Your shipped games do the talking\.

Active developer voice; proof leads\.

Build one Roblox profile that proves you ship\.

One profile\. All the proof studios need\.

Tighter, less explainer\-like\.

Join the waitlist\.

Claim your slot\.

Ownership, urgency, and product\-specific metaphor\.

Weld turns your shipped games into a trust\-verified profile\.

Ship games\. Weld turns proof into opportunity\.

Leads with user action, not product feature\.

Studios filter by role, rate, and availability\.

Studios pull you in \- no cold DMs\.

States the emotional outcome\.

Free during beta\.

Beta access\. No cost\.

Avoids SaaS pricing\-tier tone\.

Developer\-first\.

Built in LuaU country\.

Insider signal, use sparingly\.

Get hiring access\.

Scout the roster\.

Game\-industry and studio\-side language\.

## Section copy recipes

__Section__

__Developer mode copy__

__Studio mode copy__

Hero eyebrow

weld\.roster booted for builders who ship

scout view initialized

Hero headline

Your shipped games do the talking\.

Find Roblox developers with proof already attached\.

Hero subcopy

One profile\. Roles, rates, visits, availability, and verified work \- compiled into something studios can inspect\.

Pull creators by role, rate, proof, and availability without crawling Discord threads\.

Role explorer

Choose your lane\. Watch the profile recompile\.

Filter the roster like a Studio Explorer tree\.

Discord section

No more explaining yourself in DMs\.

No more hiring out of a noisy channel\.

Stats section

Proof metrics that survived shipping\.

Signals you can scout before you message\.

CTA

Claim your slot\.

Scout the roster\.

__Professional craft tie\-in  
__Nielsen Norman Group's system\-status heuristic maps directly to Weld: every user action should tell the user what happened through logs, selected states, validation, and status changes\. GOV\.UK's "start with user needs" principle maps to copy: do not write for investors; write for the Roblox developer who wants proof to speak first\.

# 07\. Color System and Design Tokens

The color system replaces dark fintech SaaS energy with recognizable Roblox Studio depth and LuaU syntax semantics\. Tokens must be named by role and used consistently across components\. Color is a state language, not surface decoration\.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## Canonical token table

__Token__

__Value__

__Semantic role__

__Primary usage__

__Do not use for__

\-\-bg

\#090b14

Base depth

Page body, hero background\.

Cards, pills, active state\.

\-\-bg2

\#0e111c

Panel depth

Cards, large panels\.

Tiny hover states\.

\-\-bg\-surface

\#131726

Nested surface

Explorer, Properties, Output header\.

Global body\.

\-\-bg\-hover

\#1a1f30

Hover/row depth

Button hover, selected row backing\.

Primary CTA fill\.

\-\-studio\-blue

\#00a2ff

Primary action/selection

CTA, active tab, selected row, focus ring\.

Generic decoration\.

\-\-studio\-blue\-dark

\#0077cc

Pressed action

Pressed buttons, active control\.

Text on dark without contrast check\.

\-\-luau\-keyword

\#c792ea

Syntax labels/tags

Keywords, badges, role labels\.

Success state\.

\-\-luau\-string

\#f78c6c

Syntax strings/values

String literals, handles, values\.

Warning unless intentionally coded\.

\-\-luau\-function

\#82aaff

Function/action

Function names, action rows\.

Body text\.

\-\-luau\-type

\#4ec9b0

Type/success

Verified, proof success, type annotations\.

Primary CTA\.

\-\-luau\-variable

\#c3e88d

Availability/live

Open status, live count, active availability\.

Long text\.

\-\-luau\-comment

\#546e7a

Metadata

Timestamps, line numbers, comments\.

Critical instructions\.

\-\-orange\-hot

\#ff5a2d

Special CTA

Claim moments, final CTA, destructive attention\.

Every button\.

\-\-cream

\#e8eaf6

Readable text on dark

Panel text and body on dark surfaces\.

Light page body text\.

\-\-terminal\-dark

\#080b18

Deep code/output surface

Output window, code block interiors\.

Normal page panels\.

## Semantic usage law

- Blue means action, focus, selected state, or ready state\.
- Teal means verification, success, valid proof, or type\-level correctness\.
- Green means live/open/available, not generic success when teal is more appropriate\.
- Purple means labels, categories, badges, and syntax keywords\.
- Orange means a high\-intent claim moment or role value, not a universal brand wash\.
- Grey/comment means metadata, timestamps, line numbers, disabled hints, and low\-emphasis context\.

## CSS root snippet

__  Root tokens__  \[CSS\]

  :root \{

    \-\-bg: \#090b14;

    \-\-bg2: \#0e111c;

    \-\-bg\-surface: \#131726;

    \-\-bg\-hover: \#1a1f30;

    \-\-terminal\-dark: \#080b18;

  

    \-\-studio\-blue: \#00a2ff;

    \-\-studio\-blue\-dark: \#0077cc;

  

    \-\-luau\-keyword: \#c792ea;

    \-\-luau\-string: \#f78c6c;

    \-\-luau\-function: \#82aaff;

    \-\-luau\-type: \#4ec9b0;

    \-\-luau\-variable: \#c3e88d;

    \-\-luau\-comment: \#546e7a;

  

    \-\-orange\-hot: \#ff5a2d;

    \-\-cream: \#e8eaf6;

  \}

## Contrast and accessibility gate

WCAG contrast thresholds are gates, not suggestions\. Normal text must meet 4\.5:1\. Large text and graphical UI indicators should meet the appropriate 3:1 threshold\. Do not round borderline values upward\. If a token pair fails, change the token pairing or weight, not the requirement\.

__Pairing__

__Usage__

__Gate__

\-\-cream on \-\-bg / \-\-bg2 / \-\-terminal\-dark

Primary dark\-surface text\.

Required to pass normal text contrast\.

\-\-luau\-comment on \-\-terminal\-dark

Timestamps, metadata, line numbers\.

Use sparingly; never critical copy without stronger contrast\.

\-\-studio\-blue on \-\-bg2

Tabs, focus, actions\.

Must remain visible in hover/focus and selected state\.

\-\-orange\-hot on \-\-bg2

Final CTA and high\-intent signals\.

Use with enough weight and contrast; avoid thin text\.

\-\-luau\-variable on \-\-bg2

Open/live status\.

Must not be sole indicator; pair with label or icon\.

# 08\. Typography, Code, and Information Density

Typography should feel like Roblox Studio panels, LuaU scripts, and developer tools\. Compact does not mean cramped; it means information\-rich, stateful, and precise\. The current generic terminal direction is replaced with Studio Output, LuaU syntax, and Explorer tree density\.

## Type roles

__Role__

__Font direction__

__Size range__

__Usage__

Display

Aptos Display / Inter / Satoshi equivalent

32\-64px desktop, 28\-40px mobile

Hero headline and major section claims\.

Body

Aptos / Inter / system sans

13\-15px

Explanatory copy and normal paragraphs\.

Panel label

System sans or mono depending on component

10\-12px

Properties labels, section headers, compact metadata\.

Mono/code

JetBrains Mono / Fira Code / Consolas

10\-13px

Output logs, LuaU editor, role tree values, command input\.

Micro

System sans or mono

9\-10px

Timestamps, line numbers, badges, tooltips\.

## Density decisions

__Element__

__Generic SaaS tendency__

__Weld target__

Body copy

15\-17px with airy spacing\.

13\-14px where appropriate; precise, compact, readable\.

Section spacing

Huge vertical slabs\.

Tighter Studio\-like rhythm: content feels like panels, not billboard sections\.

Hero profile stats

Loose cards and big gutters\.

Properties\-style rows, compact labels and values\.

Role selector

Horizontal pills\.

Explorer tree with nested child nodes and selected row\.

How It Works

Three marketing cards\.

LuaU script editor tabs with line numbers and syntax\.

## Studio Output log format

__  Hero boot sequence__  \[Output\]

  \[12:04:01\.001\]  Booting weld\.roster v2\.0\.\.\.

  \[12:04:01\.120\]  Loading developer lane\.\.\.

  \[12:04:01\.245\]  Scanning shipped work\.\.\. OK

  \[12:04:01\.471\]  Verified: 17\.3M total visits

  \[12:04:01\.620\]  Matching studio filters\.\.\.

  \[12:04:01\.781\]  READY\_FOR\_DISCOVERY

## LuaU editor snippet

__  LuaU syntax block content__  \[LuaU\]

  \-\- weld\_profile\.luau

  local WeldProfile = \{\}

  

  function WeldProfile\.build\(dev\)

    dev\.role = "Scripter"

    dev\.rate = "45 R$/hr"

    dev\.availability = "Open now"

    return WeldProfile\.verify\(dev\)

  end \-\- PROOF\_VERIFIED

## Explorer role typography

__  Explorer tree pattern__  \[Text\]

  ▼ Workspace

    ▼ Roles

      ▶ Scripter      \[open now · 45 R$/hr\]

        ├─ LUAU

        ├─ OOP

        └─ DATASTORESERVICE

      ▶ UI / UX       \[open this week · 35 R$/hr\]

      ▶ VFX           \[3 slots open · 30 R$/hr\]

      ▶ Builder       \[sprint lane · 25 R$/hr\]

      ▶ Animator      \[monthly slots · 28 R$/hr\]

__Typography QA  
__If the type surface could belong to a generic terminal startup, it is wrong\. It must look like Output, Explorer, Properties, or a LuaU script editor\.

# 09\. Layout, Depth, Grid, and Panel Architecture

Weld layout is built from layered tool panels, not marketing cards\. Depth must be consistent so the page feels like an interface with nested systems rather than a random set of dark boxes\.

## Depth levels

__Depth__

__Token__

__Role__

__Example__

0

\-\-bg

Page base and hero void\.

Body, hero background, behind all panels\.

1

\-\-bg2

Major section panels\.

Large content surfaces and CTA shells\.

2

\-\-bg\-surface

Studio chrome and nested panels\.

Output header, Properties header, Explorer panel\.

3

\-\-terminal\-dark

Deep code/output interiors\.

Output log body, script editor body\.

4

\-\-bg\-hover / selected overlay

Rows, chips, selected/focused states\.

Explorer selected row, active property row\.

## Section architecture

__Section__

__Layout requirement__

__Dynamic requirement__

Hero

Two\-panel Studio boot surface with Output left and Properties/Studio window right\.

Boot logs, profile enter, CTA command readiness\.

Role Explorer

Explorer tree panel with nested role folders and child proof nodes\.

Role selection controls global selectedRole\.

How It Works

Script editor panel with tabs and line numbers\.

Tabs and code content respond to role and audience mode\.

Discord comparison

Discord dark channel panel against Weld compile panel\.

Scroll triggers parse animation and output proof signals\.

Stats

Roblox game\-detail style counters and sparklines\.

Counters animate on intersection and metric hover affects proof rows\.

Referral/Badges

Badge unlock cards, not reward coupons\.

Unlock state responds to claim/referral progress\.

CTA

Studio Welcome card with path tiles and command input\.

Input/submit state behaves like executing a command\.

FAQ

Properties collapsible groups\.

Accordion state uses accessible disclosure pattern and logs context if needed\.

## Grid rules

- Maximum content width should feel like a Studio workspace, not a centered blog: target 1180\-1320px for standard desktop\.
- Use two\-column panels where state relationships matter: Output vs Properties, Discord vs Weld, Explorer vs Profile\.
- Do not center every section as a vertical stack\. Tool surfaces should have left/right relationships\.
- Nested panels should have visible headers, separators, rows, and selected states\.
- Use 1px dividers more often than giant drop shadows\.
- Allow mild 3D and perspective only when it reinforces Studio window metaphors\.

## Background systems

__Layer__

__Description__

__Rules__

Grid

Faint Studio viewport grid\.

Low opacity, non\-distracting, no moire on mobile\.

Luau watermark

Fragments of code at 3\-5 percent opacity\.

Behind content, pointer\-events none, masked at edges\.

Particles

Canvas dots, desktop only\.

Max 80 particles, disabled on mobile and reduced motion\.

Floating Studio window

CSS\-built tilted \.rbxl window in hero\.

Must not block primary copy or degrade performance\.

__  Depth utility CSS__  \[CSS\]

  \.surface\-depth\-1 \{ background: var\(\-\-bg2\); \}

  \.surface\-depth\-2 \{ background: var\(\-\-bg\-surface\); \}

  \.surface\-code    \{ background: var\(\-\-terminal\-dark\); \}

  \.selected\-row \{

    background: color\-mix\(in srgb, var\(\-\-studio\-blue\) 20%, transparent\);

    border\-left: 2px solid var\(\-\-studio\-blue\);

  \}

# 10\. Dynamic Component Specification Library

Every component below is specified as a dynamic contract\. Codex and Claude Code should treat these contracts as implementation requirements, not inspiration\. Each component must declare state inputs, events emitted, affected sections, motion response, responsive behavior, accessibility, forbidden variants, and QA checks\.

## Component contract template

__  Required component spec format__  \[Text\]

  Component name:

  Purpose:

  Metaphor:

  State inputs:

  Events emitted:

  Affects:

  Visual anatomy:

  Motion response:

  Responsive behavior:

  Accessibility notes:

  Forbidden variants:

  QA checklist:

  Agent prompt:

### StudioToolbarHeader

The top\-level command bar for mode, brand, search/filter, and primary action\. It replaces the rounded SaaS nav pill with a Studio toolbar surface\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Roblox Studio top toolbar with icon groups and flat bottom border\.

State inputs

audienceMode, bootStatus, claimState, compactMode\.

Events emitted

onAudienceModeChange\(mode\), onPrimaryCTAIntent\(\), onSearchFocus\(\)\.

Affects

All copy perspective, CTA copy, Output logs, StudioWelcomeCTA\.

Visual anatomy

Logo mark and wordmark; thin divider; mode tab strip; optional search/filter box; notification/avatar icons; primary CTA in Studio blue\.

Motion response

Header materializes during boot; mode tab uses compile snap; focus glow uses Studio blue\.

Responsive behavior

Desktop full toolbar\. Mobile compacts to logo, mode icon, and CTA command icon\.

Accessibility notes

Use semantic header/nav\. Mode toggle must expose pressed/selected state and keyboard control\.

Forbidden variants

Floating rounded pill nav, glassy startup navbar, generic center links\.

QA checklist

Header visible, not bulky; selected mode clear; keyboard tab order sane; no layout shift during boot\.

### StudioOutputPanel

A visible system\-status log that makes the page feel alive and grounded in Roblox Studio Output\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Roblox Studio Output window with header, filters, timestamps, severity colors, and log body\.

State inputs

logs, bootStatus, selectedRole, audienceMode, claimState, reducedMotion\.

Events emitted

None required, but can emit onFilterChange\(filter\)\.

Affects

Informs the user of every major state change\.

Visual anatomy

Header labelled Output; utility icons; filter pills ALL/PRINT/WARN/ERROR; log rows with timestamp and severity\.

Motion response

Boot lines type during initial load; new lines append with short snap; reduced motion shows rows instantly\.

Responsive behavior

Mobile shows max four critical lines; filters may collapse into a small selector\.

Accessibility notes

Use aria\-live polite for new meaningful logs; do not spam screen readers with purely decorative ticks\.

Forbidden variants

Generic Linux terminal, hacker green text, random bash prompts\.

QA checklist

Every primary action appends a relevant log; colors match severity; panel reads as Output not CLI\.

### DeveloperPropertiesPanel

Shows creator proof as inspectable structured data rather than a generic profile card\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Roblox Studio Properties inspector for DeveloperProfile\.

State inputs

selectedRole, audienceMode, selectedProofMetric, reducedMotion\.

Events emitted

onPropertyFocus\(metric\), onVerifyHover\(\), onProfileExpand\(\)\.

Affects

ProofStatsGrid, LuaUSyntaxEditor comments, Output logs\.

Visual anatomy

Panel header: Properties \- DeveloperProfile; two\-column label/value rows; alternating row shading; verified teal indicators\.

Motion response

Values scramble and resolve on role change; focused rows brighten; reduced motion snaps values\.

Responsive behavior

Stacks above/below Output on tablet; on mobile, rows become compact groups with horizontal scrolling avoided\.

Accessibility notes

Labels and values must remain text, not images\. Focusable rows need visible focus state\.

Forbidden variants

Big soft avatar card, testimonial card, lifestyle bio block\.

QA checklist

Every value updates by selectedRole; labels stable; verification not conveyed by color alone\.

### ExplorerRoleTree

Primary role\-selection control and the clearest Studio\-native interaction surface\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Roblox Studio Explorer tree: Workspace > Roles > role folders > child proof nodes\.

State inputs

selectedRole, compactMode, audienceMode\.

Events emitted

onRoleSelect\(role\), onRoleExpand\(role\), onProofMetricFocus\(metric\)\.

Affects

DeveloperPropertiesPanel, StudioOutputPanel, LuaUSyntaxEditor, ProofStatsGrid, StudioWelcomeCTA\.

Visual anatomy

Explorer header; search box; Workspace root; role folders; child nodes for rate, availability, skills, shipped work; selected row\.

Motion response

Folder expands downward; child rows stagger; selected row compile\-flashes; surrounding roles dim briefly\.

Responsive behavior

Mobile collapses to icon\-only horizontal role nodes with tap\-to\-expand details\.

Accessibility notes

Use tree/treeitem semantics only if keyboard model is implemented; otherwise use buttons with aria\-expanded\.

Forbidden variants

Flat horizontal pill chips with no hierarchy or child proof nodes\.

QA checklist

Selecting a role recompiles at least five other surfaces; keyboard navigation works; mobile tap targets are usable\.

### LuaUSyntaxEditor

Transforms How It Works into a dynamic script editor that teaches the product through executable\-looking code\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Roblox Studio Script editor with tabs, line numbers, syntax colors, and run feedback\.

State inputs

selectedRole, audienceMode, activeScriptTab, reducedMotion\.

Events emitted

onScriptTabChange\(tab\), onRunPreview\(\), onCodeLineFocus\(line\)\.

Affects

Output logs, How It Works explanation, CTA readiness copy\.

Visual anatomy

Tab bar; active tab bottom border; line\-number gutter; syntax\-highlighted LuaU; optional green play/run button\.

Motion response

Lines type on intersection; tab changes snap; run button triggers PROOF\_VERIFIED comment\.

Responsive behavior

Mobile tabs become dropdown or segmented icon selector; code wraps safely or scrolls inside editor only\.

Accessibility notes

Code block must be readable text; provide accessible label for file/tab controls\.

Forbidden variants

Bash terminal command cards, fake npm commands, generic shell prompts\.

QA checklist

Syntax uses Stage 1 tokens; line numbers aligned; selectedRole changes code content\.

### DiscordChaosPanel

Shows the problem Weld solves by rendering Discord\-like hiring noise, then letting Weld parse it\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Discord dark channel UI used only as the chaos side of the comparison\.

State inputs

parseState, audienceMode, reducedMotion\.

Events emitted

onParseStart\(\), onNoiseMessageFocus\(messageId\)\.

Affects

WeldCompilePanel, StudioOutputPanel, proof signal rows\.

Visual anatomy

Left sidebar hint; \#dev\-hiring channel; avatar, username, timestamp, message body, blocked/deleted states\.

Motion response

Panel slides from left; messages jitter subtly; parse line flashes into Weld panel; reduced motion uses instant state changes\.

Responsive behavior

Mobile stacks Discord above Weld with a clear parse arrow/transition between them\.

Accessibility notes

Do not rely on chaotic visuals only; label messages as example noise and proof parse output\.

Forbidden variants

Cute fake chat bubbles, abstract before/after cards, Discord as the whole brand identity\.

QA checklist

Discord side feels noisy; Weld side feels structured; no copyrighted logos required\.

### WeldCompilePanel

Shows noisy inputs becoming structured proof signals\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Compiler/output transform panel adjacent to Discord chaos\.

State inputs

parseState, selectedRole, proofSignals, reducedMotion\.

Events emitted

onSignalHover\(signal\), onParseComplete\(\)\.

Affects

Output logs, ProofStatsGrid, Properties proof rows\.

Visual anatomy

Header: Weld compile; input signal rows; extracted fields; result summary; proof status\.

Motion response

Rows appear as if parsed; green/teal success ticks; final ready state pulse\.

Responsive behavior

Mobile uses a compact vertical parse stack with no horizontal overflow\.

Accessibility notes

Use text labels for parsed fields and status; avoid color\-only success\.

Forbidden variants

Generic AI analysis card or magic sparkle extraction block\.

QA checklist

Signals map to real profile properties; parse animation communicates structure not decoration\.

### ProofStatsGrid

Displays shipped proof metrics with Roblox game\-detail energy\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Roblox game page stats and visit counters with sparklines\.

State inputs

selectedRole, selectedProofMetric, intersectionState, reducedMotion\.

Events emitted

onMetricFocus\(metric\), onCounterComplete\(metric\)\.

Affects

DeveloperPropertiesPanel rows, Output logs, badge unlock emphasis\.

Visual anatomy

Compact stat cards, icon, monospace number, label, mini sparkline, verification hint\.

Motion response

Count\-up on entry; sparkline draws; hover focuses related proof row\.

Responsive behavior

Two\-column mobile cards or horizontal stat strip if density remains readable\.

Accessibility notes

Numbers must include accessible labels and not be image\-only\.

Forbidden variants

Generic KPI grid with huge numbers and no relation to Roblox proof\.

QA checklist

Counters trigger once per view; no jank; selected role updates values correctly\.

### ReferralBadgeUnlockCard

Turns referral/reward content into Roblox badge\-like unlock moments\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Roblox badge award/unlock UI, adapted without official badge misuse\.

State inputs

badgeState, referralProgress, reducedMotion\.

Events emitted

onBadgeFocus\(badgeId\), onBadgeUnlock\(badgeId\)\.

Affects

Output logs and CTA confidence copy\.

Visual anatomy

Badge icon, title, criteria, unlock progress, proof/confidence text\.

Motion response

Locked to unlocked pop, green/teal border pulse, progress tick\.

Responsive behavior

Cards become compact rows with clear lock/unlock state\.

Accessibility notes

Use text status: locked, unlocked, progress\. Do not rely on icon only\.

Forbidden variants

Coupon\-looking reward cards or NFT\-style badges\.

QA checklist

Unlock state understandable; no official Roblox badge imitation\.

### StudioWelcomeCTA

Final conversion area that behaves like starting a project or executing a command\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Roblox Studio Welcome/New Project screen plus command buffer\.

State inputs

selectedRole, audienceMode, claimState, email, compactMode\.

Events emitted

onPathTileSelect\(role\), onEmailChange\(email\), onClaimSubmit\(\)\.

Affects

Output logs, ClaimSlotInput, success badge, selectedRole\.

Visual anatomy

Welcome card, role path tiles, email command input, CTA button, proof microcopy\.

Motion response

Tile selection compiles; command readiness glows; submit compresses then resolves success/error\.

Responsive behavior

Mobile path tiles become horizontal icon row; input remains full\-width and touch\-friendly\.

Accessibility notes

Input has label, error text, and clear submit status\. Enter key submits when valid\.

Forbidden variants

Generic newsletter/waitlist block\.

QA checklist

Claim flow has idle, typing, ready, submitting, success, and error states\.

### PropertiesFAQAccordion

FAQ becomes inspectable property groups instead of a simple marketing accordion\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Studio Properties collapsible groups\.

State inputs

expandedFaqGroup, audienceMode, reducedMotion\.

Events emitted

onFaqToggle\(group\), onFaqFocus\(group\)\.

Affects

Optional Output context logs\.

Visual anatomy

Group headers with disclosure icon, nested rows, compact explanatory text\.

Motion response

Rows expand with measured height; reduced motion toggles instantly\.

Responsive behavior

Single\-column mobile groups with comfortable touch targets\.

Accessibility notes

Use button controls with aria\-expanded and labelled panels\.

Forbidden variants

Generic FAQ cards with floating shadows and no Studio styling\.

QA checklist

Keyboard and screen\-reader semantics pass; nested content readable\.

### RobloxProofBadge

Indicates proof verification while avoiding false official endorsement\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Roblox trust\-badge inspired, not official Roblox\-issued verification\.

State inputs

verificationState, selectedRole\.

Events emitted

onBadgeHover\(\), onBadgeFocus\(\)\.

Affects

Tooltip, Properties verified rows, Output logs if inspection occurs\.

Visual anatomy

Small pill, teal\-blue fill, original CSS/SVG icon, text such as "Proof verified" or "Roblox proof checked"\.

Motion response

Tooltip appears on hover/focus; subtle glow if verification active\.

Responsive behavior

Tooltip becomes tap\-to\-expand help text on mobile\.

Accessibility notes

Tooltip content must be available on focus and touch\.

Forbidden variants

Official Roblox logo, official\-looking verification mark, "Roblox Verified" if it implies official endorsement\.

QA checklist

Legal/brand wording reviewed; badge meaning clear; not color\-only\.

### FloatingStudioWindow

A hero motif that creates immediate Studio recognition and screenshot value\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

CSS\-built tilted Roblox Studio window with viewport grid and Properties side panel\.

State inputs

bootStatus, scrollProgress, reducedMotion, compactMode\.

Events emitted

None required; optional onWindowHover\(\)\.

Affects

Hero depth and visual memorability\.

Visual anatomy

Title bar, macOS dots, toolbar strip, viewport grid, blocky silhouette, slim right Properties panel\.

Motion response

Breathing tilt and parallax on desktop only; reduced motion freezes it\.

Responsive behavior

Hidden or simplified on mobile if it risks crowding or performance\.

Accessibility notes

Decorative; mark aria\-hidden unless it communicates content\.

Forbidden variants

Raster screenshots of Studio, copied Roblox UI assets, heavy 3D libraries\.

QA checklist

Pure CSS or lightweight; no jank; does not compete with CTA\.

### LuauWatermarkLayer

Adds subtle Studio/LuaU texture and insider recognition across the page\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Fragments of code left open in the background of a project\.

State inputs

reducedMotion, compactMode, activeScriptTab\.

Events emitted

None required\.

Affects

Ambient brand texture\.

Visual anatomy

Absolutely positioned code fragments at 3\-5 percent opacity, slightly rotated, masked at top/bottom\.

Motion response

Optional very slow drift; disabled on reduced motion/mobile\.

Responsive behavior

Disable or heavily simplify under 640px\.

Accessibility notes

aria\-hidden; must never reduce text contrast\.

Forbidden variants

Readable distracting code wallpaper, copied proprietary snippets\.

QA checklist

Visible only when looking for it; no contrast or performance damage\.

### ParticleCanvas

A lightweight breathing layer that makes the page feel alive\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Running viewport/game\-like ambient particles behind the Studio interface\.

State inputs

reducedMotion, compactMode, viewportSize\.

Events emitted

None\.

Affects

Ambient depth only\.

Visual anatomy

Canvas behind content, max 80 particles, pointer\-events none\.

Motion response

requestAnimationFrame drift; no DOM particle creation per frame\.

Responsive behavior

0 particles on mobile; disabled with reduced motion\.

Accessibility notes

Decorative and hidden from assistive tech\.

Forbidden variants

DOM particle spam, heavy libraries, bright distracting confetti\.

QA checklist

60fps on mid\-range desktop; no battery drain; cleanup on unmount\.

### CommandButton

Button component that feels tactile and command\-like without cheap shimmer\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Studio action button / compile action\.

State inputs

claimState, disabled, variant, reducedMotion\.

Events emitted

onClick\(\), onFocus\(\), onHoverStart\(\)\.

Affects

CTA state, Output logs, form submission\.

Visual anatomy

Solid or outlined button, 1px border, Studio\-blue/Orange accent, compact label, optional command icon\.

Motion response

Hover builds glow over 200ms; click snaps to 0\.97; one\-time shimmer only if used on hover, never looping\.

Responsive behavior

Touch target at least comfortable; no hover\-only information on mobile\.

Accessibility notes

Use real button element, focus\-visible, disabled semantics\.

Forbidden variants

Continuous idle shimmer, gradient rainbow CTA, anchor pretending to be button\.

QA checklist

Focus visible; idle state calm; click feedback immediate\.

### ClaimSlotInput

Email capture that behaves like a command buffer rather than a newsletter field\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Command parameter input inside a Studio/tool surface\.

State inputs

email, claimState, selectedRole, audienceMode\.

Events emitted

onEmailChange\(email\), onValidateEmail\(\), onSubmit\(\)\.

Affects

CommandButton, StudioOutputPanel, success/error badge\.

Visual anatomy

Prompt text, input, validation hint, CTA, status message\.

Motion response

Focus glow; readiness prompt; submit compile snap; success badge unlock\.

Responsive behavior

Full\-width mobile input; large enough tap target; label remains visible or accessible\.

Accessibility notes

Input label, aria\-invalid on error, error text connected with aria\-describedby\.

Forbidden variants

Placeholder\-only email input, generic "Join waitlist" form\.

QA checklist

Valid/invalid paths tested; no data lost on role switch\.

### AudienceModeToggle

Lets users view Weld as developer or studio without splitting the product narrative\.

__Contract field__

__Specification__

Metaphor

Studio toolbar tab strip / view mode toggle\.

State inputs

audienceMode, compactMode\.

Events emitted

onAudienceModeChange\(mode\)\.

Affects

Hero copy, CTA copy, Discord section perspective, Output logs\.

Visual anatomy

Two segmented tabs with icon and label; selected tab has Studio\-blue underline or row highlight\.

Motion response

Compile snap on switch; text values update globally\.

Responsive behavior

Icon\-only with accessible labels on mobile\.

Accessibility notes

Use buttons with aria\-pressed or tabs with full tab pattern, not generic divs\.

Forbidden variants

Hidden preference buried in a dropdown on desktop\.

QA checklist

Switching mode changes at least three copy surfaces and logs the change\.

# 11\. Motion System: Boot Feel, Breathing, Compile Snap

Motion is state communication\. It should make the page feel alive like a running tool, but every movement must have a job\. If motion only looks expensive, remove it\.

## Principle 1: Boot Feel

The page loads like a tool starting\. It does not simply appear\. The sequence should feel like Studio launching, scripts compiling, and proof becoming ready for inspection\.

__Frame__

__Timing__

__Event__

__Mechanism__

0

0ms

Near\-black page, logo mark visible at low opacity\.

Initial CSS state\.

1

80ms

Single\-frame CRT/Studio boot flash\.

boot\-flash overlay keyframe\.

2

200ms

Header slides down and materializes\.

header\-enter transition\.

3

300\-800ms

Output boot lines type one by one\.

bootSequence\(\) and line cursor\.

4

900ms

Properties/profile card rotates in\.

profile\-enter keyframe\.

5

1100ms

CTA pulses once to signal readiness\.

cta\-ready\-pulse keyframe\.

__  Boot keyframes__  \[CSS\]

  @keyframes boot\-flash \{

    0% \{ opacity: 0; \}

    50% \{ opacity: 0\.9; \}

    100% \{ opacity: 0; \}

  \}

  @keyframes profile\-enter \{

    from \{ opacity: 0; transform: translateX\(\-40px\) rotateY\(\-8deg\); \}

    to   \{ opacity: 1; transform: translateX\(0\) rotateY\(0\); \}

  \}

  @keyframes cta\-ready\-pulse \{

    0%   \{ box\-shadow: 0 0 0 rgba\(0,162,255,0\); \}

    50%  \{ box\-shadow: 0 0 40px rgba\(0,162,255,0\.6\); \}

    100% \{ box\-shadow: 0 0 0 rgba\(0,162,255,0\); \}

  \}

## Principle 2: Breathing

The idle page should feel alive without demanding attention\. Breathing includes the ParticleCanvas, hero card tilt, slow Studio window motion, and count\-up proof metrics\. Breathing is disabled or simplified on mobile and reduced\-motion modes\.

__Mechanism__

__Rules__

__Performance guard__

Particle field

Max 80 desktop particles, 2\-3px dots, blue/purple at low opacity\.

Canvas only, no DOM nodes per particle, cleanup on unmount\.

Hero card tilt

Max 8deg X and 5deg Y, smoothed with lerp 0\.08\.

Mouse only; disable on touch/reduced motion\.

Animated counters

Count from 0 on viewport entry over about 1200ms\.

IntersectionObserver, one run per metric\.

Studio window drift

6s ease\-in\-out breathing tilt and mild parallax\.

Desktop only; no layout shifts\.

## Principle 3: Compile Snap

Interactions should respond sharply and briefly, like code executing\. The system should feel precise, not bouncy\. Compile Snap applies to role selection, buttons, inputs, tab switching, and proof verification\.

__Interaction__

__Required response__

__Reduced\-motion fallback__

Role click

Selected row brightens, rows dim outward, values scramble, Output logs append\.

Instant row selection, instant values, logs append\.

CTA hover

Glow builds over 200ms, one shimmer sweep if used, lift holds\.

Focus outline/glow only\.

CTA click

Scale snaps to 0\.97 for 80ms then resolves\.

No scale; pressed color state only\.

Email focus

Blue border and prompt brightening\.

Instant border/focus state\.

Code tab switch

Active border moves, code content resolves\.

Instant content switch\.

## Motion matrix

__Motion__

__Trigger__

__Duration__

__Easing__

__Fallback__

__Risk__

Boot flash

Page load

120ms

linear

Skip flash

Low

Header enter

Boot frame 2

300ms

ease\-out

Show header

Low

Boot line typing

Page load

500ms total

stepped

Show all lines

Low

Profile enter

Boot frame 4

400ms

ease\-out

Fade only

Medium

Particle field

Idle desktop

continuous

RAF

Disabled

Medium

Card tilt

Mousemove

continuous

lerp 0\.08

Disabled

Medium

Compile flash

Role select

250ms

ease\-out

Static selected row

Low

Code typing

Intersection

800\-1400ms

stepped

Full code visible

Low

Counters

Intersection

1200ms

ease\-out

Final number visible

Low

## Reduced\-motion contract

__  Reduced motion CSS__  \[CSS\]

  @media \(prefers\-reduced\-motion: reduce\) \{

    \*, \*::before, \*::after \{

      animation\-duration: 0\.001ms \!important;

      animation\-iteration\-count: 1 \!important;

      scroll\-behavior: auto \!important;

      transition\-duration: 0\.001ms \!important;

    \}

    \.particle\-canvas,

    \.parallax\-layer,

    \.cursor\-trail \{

      display: none \!important;

    \}

  \}

__Motion law  
__Never remove meaning in reduced\-motion mode\. Replace motion with immediate state changes, clearer labels, and visible focus/status states\.

# 12\. Mobile Compact Dynamic Mode

Mobile is not a stacked brochure version of desktop\. It is a compact Studio\-like interface where panels collapse, state remains connected, and high\-cost motion is removed\.

## Breakpoints

__Breakpoint__

__Mode__

__Behavior__

< 640px

Compact mobile

Icon rows, short Output logs, reduced particles, dropdown tabs, touch\-first CTA\.

640\-1024px

Tablet stacked panels

Two\-panel relationships become vertical but remain linked through state\.

1024\-1440px

Standard desktop

Full dynamic surface with two\-column panels and ambient motion\.

1440px\+

Cinematic wide

More breathing room, hero Studio window, large panel relationships\.

## Mobile transformations

__Desktop component__

__Mobile compact behavior__

__State preservation__

StudioToolbarHeader

Logo \+ mode icon \+ CTA icon; optional drawer for search\.

audienceMode remains global\.

StudioOutputPanel

Max four key logs visible; expand control for full log\.

logs array unchanged\.

DeveloperPropertiesPanel

Compact rows, two\-column if space allows; no overflowing values\.

selectedRole updates exactly as desktop\.

ExplorerRoleTree

Horizontal icon\-only role nodes; tap expands details inline\.

onRoleSelect still recompiles all surfaces\.

LuaUSyntaxEditor

Tabs become dropdown/segmented selector; code scrolls inside editor only\.

activeScriptTab remains global\.

DiscordChaosPanel

Discord and Weld panels stack with parse transition between them\.

parseState preserved\.

ProofStatsGrid

Two\-column or horizontal stat strip; counters may snap if low power\.

selectedProofMetric remains interactive\.

StudioWelcomeCTA

Path tiles become icon row; command input full width\.

claimState unchanged\.

## Mobile bans

- No horizontal page overflow\.
- No hover\-only information\.
- No particles on mobile\.
- No mouse\-follow tilt on touch devices\.
- No long boot sequence; show four highest\-value lines maximum\.
- No tiny tap targets disguised as Studio controls\.
- No hiding the CTA below excessive decorative hero content\.

## Compact section heading treatment

__  Mobile Studio\-style labels__  \[CSS\]

  \.mobile\-section\-label \{

    display: grid;

    grid\-template\-columns: auto 1fr;

    gap: 0\.75rem;

    align\-items: center;

    font\-size: 0\.68rem;

    letter\-spacing: 0\.12em;

    text\-transform: uppercase;

    color: var\(\-\-luau\-comment\);

  \}

  \.mobile\-section\-label::after \{

    content: "";

    height: 1px;

    background: rgba\(255,255,255,0\.1\);

  \}

__Mobile acceptance test  
__A phone user should still be able to select a role, see the profile recompile, inspect proof, parse Discord noise, and claim a slot\. If mobile only lets them scroll static text, it fails\.

# 13\. Accessibility and Inclusive Interaction

The page can be rebellious without being hostile\. Weld uses dense developer\-tool aesthetics, but dense does not mean inaccessible\. The interface must be keyboard\-operable, readable, reduced\-motion aware, and clear without relying only on color\.

## Core accessibility principles

__Area__

__Requirement__

__Implementation note__

Contrast

Normal text meets 4\.5:1; large text/non\-text UI indicators meet applicable 3:1 thresholds\.

Check actual token pairings and anti\-aliased text\.

Keyboard

Every interactive element reachable and visible by keyboard\.

Use real buttons/inputs/links before ARIA\.

Focus

Focus\-visible must be clear and Studio\-blue\.

Never remove outline without replacement\.

Reduced motion

Respect prefers\-reduced\-motion\.

Disable particles, parallax, tilt, large transforms; preserve state\.

ARIA

Use WAI\-ARIA patterns only when implementing the expected keyboard behavior\.

Tree, tabs, accordion each have specific models\.

Forms

Labels, validation, aria\-invalid, aria\-describedby\.

Placeholder\-only is banned\.

Color meaning

Pair color with labels/icons/text\.

Verified must have text and/or icon, not teal alone\.

Code blocks

Readable text with sufficient contrast and selectable content\.

Avoid rendering code as image\.

## Component accessibility rules

__Component__

__Minimum standard__

ExplorerRoleTree

If using ARIA tree, implement arrow\-key navigation\. Otherwise use nested buttons with aria\-expanded\.

LuaUSyntaxEditor

Tabs use tablist/tab semantics or buttons with clear selected state; code text remains accessible\.

PropertiesFAQAccordion

Header buttons expose aria\-expanded and control labelled panels\.

ClaimSlotInput

Visible label or accessible label; errors announced; submit state not only visual\.

StudioOutputPanel

Meaningful status changes may use aria\-live polite, but avoid noisy decorative log announcements\.

Tooltips

Hover, focus, and touch access\. Tooltip information cannot be essential unless reachable another way\.

## Keyboard order

1. Header logo/skip link if present\.
2. Audience mode toggle\.
3. Primary CTA/search if visible\.
4. Hero Output filters if interactive\.
5. Role explorer controls\.
6. Script tabs/run control\.
7. Discord parse/proof controls\.
8. Stats/proof metrics\.
9. CTA path tiles, email input, claim button\.
10. FAQ disclosure buttons\.

## Accessibility QA table

__Check__

__Pass condition__

__Failure example__

Tab navigation

No trap, no skipped essential controls\.

Explorer rows clickable only by mouse\.

Focus visible

Blue outline/glow visible on all dark surfaces\.

Focus removed because hover looked nicer\.

Screen reader labels

Inputs, toggles, tabs, and buttons have meaningful labels\.

Icon\-only role nodes without accessible names\.

Reduced motion

No particles, parallax, tilt, typing, or large transforms\.

Code still types character by character in reduce mode\.

Form errors

Error text explains problem and is associated with input\.

Only red border appears\.

Color\-only meaning

Status includes text or icon label\.

Open/closed shown only by green/grey\.

# 14\. Performance Budget and Technical Standards

Dynamic does not mean heavy\. The system must feel alive while protecting load speed, interaction latency, and visual stability\. If a dynamic effect risks the product feeling slow, simplify the effect\.

## Core Web Vitals gates

__Metric__

__Target gate__

__Weld implication__

LCP

<= 2\.5s at 75th percentile

Hero should not wait on large images or heavy animation libraries\.

INP

<= 200ms at 75th percentile

Interactions such as role select and claim submit must respond immediately\.

CLS

<= 0\.1 at 75th percentile

Boot animation cannot shift layout; reserve space for panels and counters\.

## Animation budget

__Constraint__

__Limit__

__Reason__

Particle count

Max 80 desktop, 0 mobile

Protect CPU and battery\.

Animation library

Vanilla CSS and requestAnimationFrame only unless explicitly approved

Avoid bundle bloat and unpredictable runtime costs\.

Frame target

60fps on mid\-range devices

Developer\-tool polish requires responsiveness\.

will\-change

Use sparingly and remove when not needed

Avoid GPU memory bloat\.

Scroll handlers

Passive listeners and RAF throttle only

No scroll jank\.

Images

Avoid large raster UI screenshots

Use CSS constructions/SVG where possible\.

Layout shifts

Reserve panel/counter dimensions

Protect CLS\.

## Engineering rules

- Prefer CSS transitions for simple opacity/transform states\.
- Use requestAnimationFrame only for canvas particles, cursor/tilt smoothing, and other true per\-frame updates\.
- Never animate width/height/layout where transform/opacity can communicate the same state\.
- Use IntersectionObserver for scroll\-triggered animations, not raw scroll polling\.
- Do not ship dynamic effects that require large dependencies unless there is a documented reason\.
- Log and validate reduced\-motion behavior in QA before launch\.

__  Animation guard helper__  \[TypeScript\]

  const prefersReducedMotion = \(\) =>

    typeof window \!== "undefined" &&

    window\.matchMedia\("\(prefers\-reduced\-motion: reduce\)"\)\.matches;

  

  export function safeAnimate\(run: \(\) => void, fallback: \(\) => void\) \{

    if \(prefersReducedMotion\(\)\) return fallback\(\);

    return run\(\);

  \}

__Performance law  
__A dynamic surface that janks feels less premium than a static surface\. Motion must be lightweight enough that users feel control, not spectacle\.

# 15\. Roblox\-Inspired Brand Safety

Weld can be deeply Roblox Studio\-inspired without implying that Roblox endorses, operates, verifies, or officially certifies Weld\. The master system separates interface inspiration from brand\-asset misuse\.

## Safe direction

- Use Roblox Studio\-inspired panel metaphors: Output, Explorer, Properties, script tabs, viewport grids\.
- Use original CSS\-drawn icons that evoke generic tool concepts, not copied Roblox marks\.
- Use the word Roblox descriptively where needed, such as "Roblox developers" and "Roblox proof"\.
- Use proof language tied to the actual verification method, such as "Proof verified via Roblox OAuth"\.
- Make brand\-affiliation disclaimers clear in legal/footer copy if needed\.

## Risky direction

- Using the official Roblox logo or Roblox Tilt as a general UI mark\.
- Designing a badge that looks like it was issued by Roblox\.
- Saying "Roblox Verified" without context, especially next to an official\-looking mark\.
- Copying exact UI assets or screenshots instead of building original inspired surfaces\.
- Using language that suggests Weld is an official Roblox product or partner when it is not\.

__Element__

__Preferred wording/design__

__Avoid__

Verification badge

Roblox proof verified; Proof checked via Roblox OAuth; Game data verified\.

Roblox Verified with official\-looking star/check\.

Hero Studio window

CSS\-built inspired mock window labelled Weld Studio \- Developer\.rbxl\.

Actual Roblox Studio screenshot or copied toolbar icons\.

Icons

Original simple geometric Studio\-like icons\.

Official Roblox logo/tilt or downloaded icon set if rights unclear\.

Footer

Independent tool; not affiliated with Roblox unless that is true\.

Official\-sounding partnership wording\.

__Brand\-safety rule  
__Be native to the developer workflow, not dependent on official brand marks\. The strongest signal is a faithful tool metaphor, not logo usage\.
