# 17\. Snippet Library

The snippets below are designed to reduce agent improvisation\. They should be copied into the codebase only after adapting filenames and project conventions\.

## Weld component contract comment

__  Weld component contract comment__  \[TypeScript\]

  /\*\*

   \* Weld Component Contract

   \* Metaphor: Roblox Studio Output panel

   \* Dynamic rule: this component must read or emit page state\.

   \* Must preserve:

   \* \- Studio\-dark panel nesting

   \* \- Timestamped log rows

   \* \- Severity color mapping

   \* \- Reduced\-motion fallback

   \* Must avoid:

   \* \- Generic terminal styling

   \* \- Green hacker aesthetic

   \* \- Looping shimmer

   \*/

## Output log appender

__  Output log appender__  \[TypeScript\]

  function timestampNow\(\) \{

    const d = new Date\(\);

    const h = String\(d\.getHours\(\)\)\.padStart\(2, "0"\);

    const m = String\(d\.getMinutes\(\)\)\.padStart\(2, "0"\);

    const s = String\(d\.getSeconds\(\)\)\.padStart\(2, "0"\);

    const ms = String\(d\.getMilliseconds\(\)\)\.padStart\(3, "0"\);

    return \`\[$\{h\}:$\{m\}:$\{s\}\.$\{ms\}\]\`;

  \}

  

  function appendOutputLog\(message: string, severity: WeldOutputLog\["severity"\] = "info"\) \{

    setLogs\(prev => \[

      \.\.\.prev\.slice\(\-11\),

      \{ id: crypto\.randomUUID\(\), timestamp: timestampNow\(\), severity, message \}

    \]\);

  \}

## Command button hover without idle shimmer

__  Command button hover without idle shimmer__  \[CSS\]

  \.command\-button \{

    position: relative;

    border: 1px solid rgba\(255,255,255,0\.12\);

    transition: transform 200ms ease, box\-shadow 200ms ease, border\-color 200ms ease;

  \}

  \.command\-button:hover,

  \.command\-button:focus\-visible \{

    transform: translateY\(\-2px\) scale\(1\.03\);

    box\-shadow:

      0 0 32px rgba\(0, 162, 255, 0\.45\),

      0 8px 24px rgba\(0, 0, 0, 0\.3\);

    border\-color: rgba\(0, 162, 255, 0\.6\);

  \}

  \.command\-button:active \{

    transform: translateY\(0\) scale\(0\.97\);

  \}

## Explorer selected row

__  Explorer selected row__  \[CSS\]

  \.explorer\-row \{

    display: grid;

    grid\-template\-columns: 1rem minmax\(0, 1fr\) auto;

    align\-items: center;

    min\-height: 28px;

    padding: 0 0\.5rem;

    font: 11px/1\.4 var\(\-\-font\-mono\);

    color: var\(\-\-cream\);

  \}

  \.explorer\-row\[data\-selected="true"\] \{

    background: color\-mix\(in srgb, var\(\-\-studio\-blue\) 20%, transparent\);

    border\-left: 2px solid var\(\-\-studio\-blue\);

  \}

  \.explorer\-row:focus\-visible \{

    outline: 2px solid var\(\-\-studio\-blue\);

    outline\-offset: \-2px;

  \}

## Role selection handler

__  Role selection handler__  \[TypeScript\]

  function handleRoleSelect\(role: WeldRole\) \{

    setSelectedRole\(role\);

    appendOutputLog\(\`Role filter changed: $\{weldRoles\[role\]\.label\}\`, "info"\);

    appendOutputLog\("Recompiling developer profile\.\.\.", "success"\);

    setActiveScriptTab\("profile"\);

    setSelectedProofMetric\("visits"\);

    if \(\!reducedMotion\) compileFlash\(role\);

  \}

## Accessible accordion pattern

__  Accessible accordion pattern__  \[TSX\]

  <button

    type="button"

    className="property\-group\-header"

    aria\-expanded=\{isOpen\}

    aria\-controls=\{\`faq\-panel\-$\{id\}\`\}

    id=\{\`faq\-button\-$\{id\}\`\}

    onClick=\{\(\) => setOpen\(id\)\}

  >

    <span aria\-hidden>\{isOpen ? "▼" : "▶"\}</span>

    \{title\}

  </button>

  <div

    id=\{\`faq\-panel\-$\{id\}\`\}

    role="region"

    aria\-labelledby=\{\`faq\-button\-$\{id\}\`\}

    hidden=\{\!isOpen\}

  >

    \{children\}

  </div>

## Particle canvas guard

__  Particle canvas guard__  \[TypeScript\]

  useEffect\(\(\) => \{

    if \(reducedMotion || compactMode\) return;

    const canvas = canvasRef\.current;

    if \(\!canvas\) return;

    let frame = 0;

    let raf = 0;

    const particles = createParticlePool\(80, canvas\);

    const tick = \(\) => \{

      frame \+= 1;

      drawParticles\(canvas, particles, frame\);

      raf = requestAnimationFrame\(tick\);

    \};

    raf = requestAnimationFrame\(tick\);

    return \(\) => cancelAnimationFrame\(raf\);

  \}, \[reducedMotion, compactMode\]\);

## Dynamic QA Playwright outline

__  Dynamic QA Playwright outline__  \[TypeScript\]

  test\("role selection recompiles connected surfaces", async \(\{ page \}\) => \{

    await page\.goto\("/"\);

    await page\.getByRole\("button", \{ name: /Builder/i \}\)\.click\(\);

    await expect\(page\.getByText\(/Role filter changed: Builder/i\)\)\.toBeVisible\(\);

    await expect\(page\.getByText\(/Properties \- DeveloperProfile/i\)\)\.toBeVisible\(\);

    await expect\(page\.getByText\(/weld\_profile\.luau/i\)\)\.toBeVisible\(\);

    await expect\(page\.getByText\(/Claim your Builder slot/i\)\)\.toBeVisible\(\);

  \}\);

# 18\. Agent Prompt Pack for Codex and Claude Code

These prompts are designed to be pasted into coding agents before implementation or review\. They enforce dynamic\-first behavior and prevent generic SaaS drift\.

## Global implementation prompt

__  Global implementation prompt__  \[Agent Prompt\]

  You are implementing Weld's landing page using the Weld Frontend Design Master System\.

  

  Weld is dynamic\-first, not hybrid\. Do not implement static marketing sections with decorative animation\. Every major section must read state, emit events, respond to scroll/input, or affect another section\.

  

  Before editing code:

  1\. Identify the section being changed\.

  2\. Identify its Roblox Studio / LuaU / Discord\-chaos metaphor\.

  3\. Identify the state it reads\.

  4\. Identify the events it emits\.

  5\. Identify the other components it affects\.

  6\. Identify reduced\-motion and mobile behavior\.

  

  Hard bans:

  \- Generic SaaS cards

  \- Continuous shimmer

  \- Generic terminal styling

  \- Static role chips

  \- Static Discord comparison

  \- Static waitlist form

  \- Large animation libraries unless approved

  

  After editing:

  1\. List files changed\.

  2\. List design rules preserved\.

  3\. List any deviations\.

  4\. Provide QA steps\.

## Codex component implementation prompt

__  Codex component implementation prompt__  \[Agent Prompt\]

  Implement the specified Weld component using the master design system\.

  

  Component: \[component name\]

  Required metaphor: \[Studio Output / Properties / Explorer / LuaU Script Editor / etc\.\]

  State inputs: \[state\]

  Events emitted: \[events\]

  Other components affected: \[components\]

  

  Constraints:

  \- Use existing tokens where possible\.

  \- No new animation library\.

  \- Respect prefers\-reduced\-motion\.

  \- Keep mobile compact mode intact\.

  \- Avoid generic marketing\-card styling\.

  \- Include a small QA checklist in the final response\.

  

  Reject your own implementation if the component only displays static text/cards\.

## Claude Code review prompt

__  Claude Code review prompt__  \[Agent Prompt\]

  Review this implementation against the Weld Frontend Design Master System\.

  

  Check for:

  \- Generic SaaS drift

  \- Static/hybrid behavior instead of dynamic\-first behavior

  \- Incorrect token usage

  \- Missing reduced\-motion fallbacks

  \- Weak Roblox Studio metaphor

  \- Overuse of animation

  \- Mobile layout regression

  \- Accessibility failures

  \- Copy that sounds like startup marketing

  \- Interactions that do not affect other components

  

  Return:

  1\. Pass/fail summary\.

  2\. Specific violations\.

  3\. Exact code\-level fixes\.

  4\. Priority order\.

  5\. Any acceptance criteria that remain unmet\.

## Copy audit prompt

__  Copy audit prompt__  \[Agent Prompt\]

  Audit this Weld landing page copy\.

  

  Replace generic SaaS language with concise Roblox developer\-native copy\.

  Prefer: ship, proof, scout, claim, compile, inspect, verify, roster, slot, lane\.

  Avoid: unlock, empower, seamless, revolutionary, creator\-first, join waitlist, get discovered\.

  

  Return a table:

  Current copy | Replacement | Reason | Risk | Approved?

## Dynamic regression prompt

__  Dynamic regression prompt__  \[Agent Prompt\]

  Perform a dynamic\-first regression review\.

  

  For each major section, answer:

  1\. What state does it read?

  2\. What events does it emit?

  3\. What other sections does it affect?

  4\. What motion communicates the state change?

  5\. What is the reduced\-motion fallback?

  6\. What is the mobile compact behavior?

  

  Fail any section that only displays static content or uses animation only as decoration\.

## Accessibility review prompt

__  Accessibility review prompt__  \[Agent Prompt\]

  Review the Weld implementation for accessibility\.

  

  Check:

  \- Keyboard order and focus\-visible states

  \- Real buttons/inputs/links instead of clickable divs

  \- ARIA only where patterns are fully implemented

  \- Contrast on token pairings

  \- Reduced\-motion behavior

  \- Error states on the claim form

  \- Tooltip access on hover, focus, and touch

  \- Color not used as the only status signal

  

  Return critical blockers first, then improvements\.

# 19\. Dynamic QA Rubric

This rubric turns taste into acceptance criteria\. A section can look attractive and still fail if it behaves like a static/hybrid landing page\.

## Scoring model

__Category__

__0 points__

__1 point__

__2 points__

Dynamic state

No state\.

Local state only\.

Reads/emits shared state and affects other sections\.

Metaphor strength

Generic SaaS\.

Lightly themed\.

Clearly Studio/LuaU/Discord/proof\-native\.

Motion purpose

No motion or decorative only\.

Local feedback\.

Motion communicates state/change/result\.

Accessibility

Keyboard/contrast issues\.

Mostly usable but missing details\.

Keyboard, contrast, labels, reduced motion pass\.

Mobile compact

Stacked static page\.

Responsive but less dynamic\.

Compact mode preserves key interactions\.

Copy voice

Generic startup copy\.

Mostly right with weak phrases\.

Proof\-led, concise, developer\-native\.

## Launch gates

__Gate__

__Required result__

Hero dynamic gate

Boot logs, Properties panel, CTA command state, and role\-relevant proof visible on first screen\.

Role recompile gate

Selecting a role updates at least Properties, Output, code, stats, and CTA\.

Discord parse gate

Discord chaos transforms into structured proof, not static before/after\.

Reduced\-motion gate

All meaning preserved with major motion disabled\.

Mobile compact gate

No horizontal overflow; core interactions remain usable\.

Brand gate

No official\-looking Roblox endorsement or unauthorized marks\.

Performance gate

No heavy animation library, no mobile particles, no layout\-shifting boot\.

Copy gate

No banned SaaS phrases in visible copy\.

## Section\-level checklist

__Section__

__Must pass__

Header

Looks like toolbar, toggles audience mode, affects global copy, keyboard accessible\.

Hero Output

Appends meaningful logs and reads boot/interaction state\.

Hero Properties

Rows update by role and show verified proof values\.

Role Explorer

Controls selectedRole and triggers cross\-section recompile\.

How It Works

LuaU editor tabs update by role/mode; not terminal commands\.

Discord

Noise\-to\-proof transformation is interactive/stateful\.

Stats

Counters/sparklines animate or snap; metric focus affects proof rows\.

Referral/Badges

Unlocks are stateful and accessible\.

CTA

Command input has full claim state machine\.

FAQ

Properties\-style accordion with accessible disclosure behavior\.

__Hard fail rule  
__Any major section that could be dropped into a random AI SaaS, fintech dashboard, or productivity landing page without modification fails the Weld design system\.

# 20\. Implementation Roadmap

The recommended roadmap builds from global state and tokens to dynamic components, then adds motion, motifs, QA, and copy polish\. This order avoids expensive refactors and keeps the page dynamic\-first from the beginning\.

## Recommended build phases

__Phase__

__Work__

__Dependencies__

__Acceptance criteria__

0\. Audit

Inventory existing components, copy, animations, and CSS variables\.

Current codebase\.

List static/hybrid failures and files to touch\.

1\. Tokens

Implement Stage 1 root tokens and semantic usages\.

None\.

All surfaces use Studio Dark/Blue/LuaU tokens\.

2\. State core

Add WeldPageState, role config, output log model, reduced\-motion hook\.

Tokens\.

Role selection can update shared state in a demo\.

3\. Hero dynamic rebuild

Output panel, Properties panel, boot sequence, CTA ready state\.

State core\.

First screen feels like Studio booting\.

4\. Role Explorer

Replace role chips with Explorer tree and cross\-section recompile\.

State core, hero\.

Role changes update at least five surfaces\.

5\. LuaU editor

Replace How It Works cards with dynamic script tabs\.

Tokens, state\.

Code updates by role/mode and uses syntax colors\.

6\. Discord parse

Build chaos panel and Weld compile panel\.

Output logs, state\.

Scroll triggers parse narrative and proof signals\.

7\. Stats/badges

Roblox\-style stats, sparklines, badge unlocks\.

Role data\.

Metrics animate/snap and connect to proof rows\.

8\. CTA command flow

Studio Welcome CTA, path tiles, command input states\.

State core, role data\.

Claim flow has idle/focus/typing/ready/submitting/success/error\.

9\. Motion polish

Boot, Breathing, Compile Snap, particles, tilt, reduced motion\.

Components stable\.

Motion communicates state and passes performance budget\.

10\. Mobile compact

Icon role nodes, short logs, script dropdown, no particles\.

Components stable\.

Mobile preserves interactions with no overflow\.

11\. Copy and brand QA

Purge SaaS copy, brand\-safety review\.

Near\-final UI\.

No banned phrases, no official\-looking endorsement\.

12\. Final QA

Accessibility, performance, visual QA, dynamic regression\.

Complete implementation\.

Launch gates pass\.

## Risk register

__Risk__

__Impact__

__Mitigation__

Agents implement static components because it is faster\.

Hybrid page, weak product feel\.

Use dynamic component contracts and review prompt\.

Motion becomes decorative/heavy\.

Jank and lost credibility\.

Require state purpose and performance budget for every animation\.

Roblox\-inspired visuals imply endorsement\.

Brand/legal ambiguity\.

Use original assets and proof wording\.

Mobile becomes stacked brochure\.

Dynamic promise lost for many users\.

Build compact mode as a first\-class state\.

Copy drifts to SaaS language\.

Audience loses trust\.

Copy audit sheet and banned phrase gate\.

Accessibility treated as later fix\.

Rework and exclusion\.

Include ARIA/keyboard/focus/reduced\-motion per component contract\.

## Definition of done

- Every major section has a defined metaphor and a state contract\.
- Every major section either reads shared state, emits an event, or both\.
- Role selection recompiles profile, code, stats, logs, and CTA\.
- Discord section demonstrates noise\-to\-proof transformation dynamically\.
- CTA behaves like command execution, not a waitlist form\.
- Reduced\-motion mode preserves meaning\.
- Mobile compact mode remains interactive\.
- No visible copy uses banned SaaS phrases\.
- No official Roblox mark is misused\.
- Core performance and accessibility gates are met\.

# 21\. Appendices and References

## Glossary

__Term__

__Meaning in Weld__

Dynamic\-first

The page behaves as a connected stateful interface rather than a static brochure\.

Hybrid

A weak middle ground where some elements are interactive but major sections remain static\. Rejected\.

Boot Feel

Initial load sequence that makes the page feel like a tool starting\.

Breathing

Subtle idle motion that suggests a live system\.

Compile Snap

Sharp interactive feedback that communicates state changes\.

Proof row

A structured line of evidence such as visits, shipped games, skills, response time, or availability\.

Studio\-native

Inspired by Roblox Studio workflow surfaces and developer mental models\.

Scoutable proof

Information structured so studios can filter and trust it before starting a conversation\.

## Source stage files

__File__

__Used for__

stage1\_color\_system\.docx

Studio Dark, Studio Blue, LuaU syntax palette, token roles, and implementation sequence\.

stage2\_typography\.docx

LuaU code aesthetic, Studio Output panel, Explorer typography, density, and shimmer removal\.

stage3\_animations\.docx

Boot Feel, Breathing, Compile Snap, animation utilities, and performance rules\.

stage4\_components\.docx

Section\-by\-section Roblox\-native metaphor map and component redesign direction\.

stage5\_motifs\.docx

Studio window, Luau watermark, badge/polish motifs, mobile compact mode, copy audit, and stats styling\.

## External standards and references

__Reference__

__Used for__

__URL__

W3C Design Tokens Community Group

Design token standardization and cross\-tool design\-system thinking\.

https://www\.w3\.org/community/design\-tokens/

Design Tokens Community Group technical report 2025\.10

Production\-oriented token structure and design\-system portability\.

https://www\.designtokens\.org/tr/2025\.10/

W3C WCAG 2\.2 Understanding SC 1\.4\.3 Contrast Minimum

Contrast thresholds and non\-rounding guidance\.

https://www\.w3\.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/contrast\-minimum

MDN prefers\-reduced\-motion

Reduced\-motion user preference and vestibular trigger context\.

https://developer\.mozilla\.org/en\-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/At\-rules/%40media/prefers\-reduced\-motion

web\.dev Core Web Vitals thresholds

LCP, INP, and CLS performance gates\.

https://web\.dev/articles/defining\-core\-web\-vitals\-thresholds

WAI\-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide

Accessible widget patterns for tabs, accordions, and tree views\.

https://www\.w3\.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/

Roblox Name and Logo Community Usage Guidelines

Brand\-safety constraints for Roblox marks and promotional usage\.

https://en\.help\.roblox\.com/hc/en\-us/articles/115001708126\-Roblox\-Name\-and\-Logo\-Community\-Usage\-Guidelines

Nielsen Norman Group: 10 Usability Heuristics

System status, real\-world language, consistency, error prevention, and minimalist focus\.

https://www\.nngroup\.com/articles/ten\-usability\-heuristics/

GOV\.UK Government Design Principles

User needs, data, inclusion, consistency, and service\-over\-website thinking\.

https://www\.gov\.uk/guidance/government\-design\-principles

__Owner__

__Review date__

YYYY\-MM\-DD

Example: use Proof verified instead of Roblox Verified\.

Avoid false official endorsement\.

Official\-looking badge, no badge\.

Design lead

YYYY\-MM\-DD

YYYY\-MM\-DD

Example: no animation library for particles\.

Performance budget and bundle control\.

GSAP, Framer Motion\.

Frontend lead

YYYY\-MM\-DD

## Final doctrine checkpoint

__Final line  
__The finished Weld page should feel like a live Roblox Studio\-native interface that proves the product by behaving like the product\. If it becomes a beautiful static brochure, it has failed\.
